body.thing: the default test suite never drives live inference, and the guard admitting a live run probes CAPABILITY not reachability
body.intention: I intend that the default test suite never drives a live shared service, and that any guard deciding whether it may do so probes the CAPABILITY THE SERVICE EXISTS FOR — not merely that it answers the phone. A probe that reads REACHABILITY to decide CAPACITY reports a saturated service as healthy and is indistinguishable from no probe at all.

=========================== short test summary info ============================
FAILED tests/inference/test_live_tests_are_opt_in.py::test_the_default_config_deselects_live_marked_tests
1 failed in 0.50s

body.narrative: RED: with addopts = '--import-mode=importlib' the default pytest configuration COLLECTS a @pytest.mark.live test (asserted on a synthetic live-marked file, so the red cannot be faked by an import error elsewhere) -> AssertionError. GREEN: addopts gains -m 'not live' -> the live-marked test is deselected while the ordinary one is still collected (both halves asserted, so a config that collects NOTHING cannot pass). -m live still selects it. Separately the DS smoke's skip guard moved from socket.create_connection() to tests/support/live_guard.can_infer(), a real one-token completion. Measured: a default-mode run now opens ZERO sockets to Hex (0 of 40 one-second samples), in 30s.
body.why: A registered-but-never-deselected marker is decoration. pytest tests/ fired a live 24B multi-turn build at Hex on EVERY run; under contention each took hours, never exited, never released its socket, and nine piled up — which then read as a broken server, twice. The only guard was a bare TCP connect, and reachability is exactly the property that stays true while capacity is zero.
